qualifying_org_definition: Implicit federal standard (501(c)(3)/c(4), gov agency, place of worship); no Nebraska-specific restriction found. Nebraska DHHS describes qualifying work as including 'unpaid/volunteer work' without limiting to specific org types beyond federal baseline.
verification_instrument: Nebraska DHHS 'Work Verification Request' form (Volunteer Verify ABAWDx, updated 2/13/2019; Spanish version also exists dated same day) — DHHS initiates and sends to the organization; org representative confirms hours per week, work type (unpaid/volunteer, in-kind, or paid), signs with name/title/phone/date, returns to DHHS by fax (#402-742-2351) / mail (P.O. Box 2992, Omaha NE 68172) / email (DHHS.ANDICenter@nebraska.gov). The form header states 'we need to verify the following information,' confirming DHHS — not the recipient — initiates contact with the organization. No state form number printed on form. Form confirmed still live and downloadable as of June 2026.
hours_formula_detail: OBBBA FAQ Q16 (Dec 10, 2025): 'must either work, participate in an employment or training activity, or volunteer in the community at least 20 hours per week or 80 hours per month.' Follows federal 7 CFR 273.24 baseline exactly.
accepts_outofstate_detail: No Nebraska-specific restriction on the geographic location of qualifying volunteer organizations was found in any reviewed source (OBBBA FAQ, FY2026 E&T State Plan, Title 475 chapter listing, Volunteer Verify ABAWDx form, DHHS SNAP pages). The Volunteer Verify ABAWDx form asks only for org rep name/title/phone — no in-state registration requirement. Federal default under 7 CFR 273.24(a)(2)(iii) permits any qualifying nonprofit regardless of state of incorporation; absent Nebraska restriction, out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should qualify operationally.
geographic_waivers: none — fully enforced statewide. Nebraska has no active ABAWD geographic waiver. The FNA waiver list as of 2026 shows no Nebraska entries. Nebraska DHHS OBBBA FAQ (Dec 2025) confirms statewide enforcement of ABAWD work requirements with no county exceptions.
submission_deadline: Changes applied at next semi-annual recertification or when other case updates occur (per OBBBA FAQ Q2/Q5). Recipient must report drop below 20 hours/week promptly; no specific monthly deadline documented. Verification form returned by org to DHHS via fax/mail/email on DHHS request.
notes:
Nebraska enforces ABAWD statewide (no waiver) with the age range expanded to 18-64 effective October 2025 under OBBBA. Volunteer work explicitly qualifies. The operative verification instrument is a DHHS-initiated 'Work Verification Request' (Volunteer Verify ABAWDx, updated 2/13/2019): DHHS sends the form to the volunteer organization, and the org representative confirms hours and signs — the recipient does not self-report hours directly, so hours_reporting is set to county_determined (caseworker/state-driven). No county assignment, EDP, or MOU prestep was found; however, the DHHS-initiated verification letter (rather than a recipient-held org-signed form like CA's CF 888) means the lane is not fully clean — it requires caseworker action to contact the org, placing this at Tier 2. Confidence is medium because the form is dated 2019 and post-OBBBA 2025 implementing guidance (Title 475 update) was not readable in full.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted adversarial re-search targeting: (1) mandatory E&T enrollment before volunteering; (2) approved volunteer site lists; (3) remote/online volunteer eligibility; (4) post-OBBBA verification workflow changes; (5) Nebraska SNAP E&T FY2026 State Plan. Key findings: Nebraska SNAP Next Step E&T is explicitly voluntary per multiple sources — no mandatory E&T enrollment or assignment is required before a recipient's volunteer hours begin to count toward the ABAWD requirement. This confirms required_prestep=none. No evidence of a state-maintained approved volunteer site list was found across four search angles; qualifying org definition appears to follow the federal baseline. The form workflow was further confirmed: the Volunteer Verify ABAWDx form header language states 'The above individual has applied for or receives assistance from our Agency. In order to determine eligibility, we need to verify the following information' — establishing that DHHS/caseworker initiates the form and sends it to the org, and the org returns it by fax/mail/email to DHHS.ANDICenter@nebraska.gov. A Spanish-language version of the form exists (Volunteer Verif ABAWD-Spanish.pdf, same 2/13/2019 date). The Nebraska SNAP E&T FY2026 State Plan PDF was located (dhhs.ne.gov) but is a compressed/encrypted PDF not readable via WebFetch; its existence is confirmed. Title 475 Chapter 1 was amended 12/24/2025, suggesting post-OBBBA updates exist but the text was not accessible. Remote/online volunteer stance remains unknown — no Nebraska-specific guidance found, and federal SNAP rules are silent on this distinction. Tier 2 classification is affirmed: volunteer lane is open with no prestep, but verification is caseworker-initiated to the org rather than self-reported, which is the operative hoop. Confidence remains medium because: (a) remote_stance is unresolved; (b) the post-OBBBA Title 475 update was not text-readable; (c) form dates are 2019 and it is unknown whether the workflow changed post-OBBBA. No adversarial evidence was found to downgrade or upgrade the tier.

FINAL PASS (2026-06-21): Successfully extracted text from four previously unreadable PDFs using pypdf. (1) Nebraska DHHS OBBBA FAQ (updated December 10, 2025, 7 pages): Q16 explicitly states 'Individuals subject to the ABAWD work requirement must either work, participate in an employment or training activity, or volunteer in the community at least 20 hours per week or 80 hours per month.' This is the most authoritative post-OBBBA public-facing DHHS statement on the volunteer lane — current as of December 2025 and confirming the lane remains open under the expanded age rules. (2) FY2026 SNAP E&T State Plan (139 pages, submitted 09/29/2025): confirms Nebraska's E&T is 100% voluntary (0 mandatory participants; all 24,000 work registrants are exempted from mandatory E&T; 2,500 voluntary participants anticipated); Nebraska pledges to offer qualifying activities under 7 CFR 273.24 to all eligible ABAWDs; no state-specific restriction on volunteer org type or on independent (outside-E&T) volunteer work found. (3) SNAP E&T State Plan Amendment FY26 (219 pages): identical findings — confirms E&T voluntary-only structure, ABAWD counts unchanged. (4) Volunteer Verify ABAWDx form itself confirmed still downloadable and unchanged: single-page DHHS-initiated form listing 'Unpaid/volunteer work' as a qualifying work type; return address is fax 402-742-2351 or DHHS.ANDICenter@nebraska.gov. No new post-OBBBA version of the form was found, but the OBBBA FAQ confirms the volunteer lane is intact under the new rules. Remote/online stance: no Nebraska-specific guidance found in any of the four documents; the federal SNAP rules do not address remote volunteer work; remote_stance updated from 'unknown' to 'silent' (Nebraska has imposed no restriction and has not affirmatively addressed the question). Confidence upgraded to HIGH: (a) general lane = yes confirmed by post-OBBBA official DHHS FAQ; (b) required_prestep = none confirmed by E&T voluntary-only structure in state plan; (c) hours_reporting = county_determined confirmed by DHHS-initiated verification form; (d) multiple independent primary sources (OBBBA FAQ, FY2026 State Plan, Volunteer Verify form) all consistent; (e) adversarial search found no EDP/MOU/registration/approved-site-list requirement; (f) primary post-OBBBA policy source (OBBBA FAQ, Dec 2025) successfully read and explicitly confirms the lane. Residual unknowns (remote_stance = silent; no new form version post-2019) are legitimate non-confidence-blocking findings, not material gaps.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): Added five new dimension groups. (1) Hours formula: OBBBA FAQ Q16 (Dec 2025) confirms federal baseline exactly — '80 hours per month or 20 hours per week'; hours_formula=federal_80, monthly_hours_required=80. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: No Nebraska-specific restriction found in any source; the Volunteer Verify ABAWDx form (text-extracted) asks only for org rep name/title/phone with no in-state registration requirement; accepts_outofstate_nonprofit=silent per federal default. (3) Geographic waivers: none — statewide enforcement confirmed; FNA waivers page shows no Nebraska entries for 2025-2029. (4) Submission UX: the Volunteer Verify form is DHHS-initiated and returns via fax/mail/email to DHHS; recipient reporting follows recertification cadence per OBBBA FAQ Q2/Q5 ('changes applied at next recertification'); no specific monthly deadline documented; submission_mechanism=[fax,mail,email], reporting_cadence=at_recertification. (5) Form fields: text-extracted form reveals: NAME (recipient), MC# (case number), hours per week (hours_by_day_or_week), work type (activity_description), org rep Name/Title/Phone/Date (supervisor_name, org_phone, org_signature implied). No org_name, org_address, org_EIN, recipient_DOB, recipient_address, notarization, or period_end_date fields present on the form; period_start_date captured as 'starting [date]'. Form is minimal by design.
